message.content:
@startuml
skinparam classAttributeIconSize 0

class IBaseRepository {
}

note right of IBaseRepository::T : T represents an entity type inheriting from BaseEntity


IBaseRepository <|-- BaseRepository : implements<T>

class "C" <<(G,#9BC2E6)>> BaseRepository #LightGreen{ 
    + BaseRepository()  
    + BaseRepository(context:ApplicationContext)   
    «virtual»«async» Create(obj:T): Task<T>    
    «virtual»«async» Update(id:long,obj:T):Task<T>     
    «virtual»«async» Delete(id:long):Task<bool>      
    «virtual»«async» Get():Task<List<T>>       
    EntryExists(id:long):bool        
    CompareByReflection(objectToCompare,comparatorObject,string propertyName,value object ): bool         
 } 

 note left of BaseRepository ::_context:_context is used to access DbContext for CRUD operations on entities in memory or DBMS system like SQL Server etc.. It's initialized through constructor injection pattern which allows us flexibility while testing our application logic without actually connecting any real databases during unit tests phase itself! This makes development process much easier since we don't need actual connection strings nor setup complex environments just yet - all done within mock objects created specifically designed around these scenarios where needed most often than not anyway ;) So let me show how easy this could be... First thing first though -> create new instance variable called "_ctx", then pass along whatever value comes back after calling 'new ApplicationContext()' inside parentheses following keyword ‘this’ so now whenever someone wants something related directly connected via said property name they'll know exactly what needs doing next step forward towards achieving goal set forth earlier above
